reasoning: Magnus Carlsen has continuously held the #1 FIDE classical rating spot since 2011. The gap between Carlsen and the world #2 player typically hovers around 30 to 50 Elo points. The timeframe between the two comparison dates (November 2025 and February 2026) is only three months. In classical chess at the super-GM level, closing a rating gap of that magnitude in just three months is extraordinarily difficult and historically unprecedented without Carlsen himself having a disastrous tournament. Furthermore, even if Carlsen were somehow ranked #2 in November 2025, he would only need to be #1 or #2 in February 2026 to satisfy the "as high or higher" criteria. Given his ongoing dominance and the extremely short interval, the probability of him maintaining his rank is exceptionally high.
